@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-4pv","title":"bd export only outputs 1 issue after auto-import corrupts database","description":"When auto-import runs and purges issues (due to git history backfill bug), subsequent 'bd export' only exports 1 issue even though the database should have many.

Reproduction:
1. Have issues.jsonl with 55 issues
2. Auto-import triggers and purges all issues via git history backfill
3. Run 'bd export' - only exports 1 issue (the last one created before corruption)

The database gets into an inconsistent state where most issues are purged but export doesn't realize this.

Workaround: Rebuild database from scratch with 'rm .beads/beads.db && bd init --prefix bd'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T22:28:40.828866-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T22:50:35.036227-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T22:50:35.036227-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-4t7","title":"Auto-import runs during --no-auto-import operations via stats/ready commands","description":"Even when using --no-auto-import flag, certain commands like 'bd stats' and 'bd ready' still trigger auto-import internally, which can cause the git history backfill bug to corrupt data.

Example:
  bd stats --no-auto-import
  # Still prints 'Purged bd-xxx (recovered from git history...)'

The flag should completely disable auto-import for the command, but it appears some code paths still trigger it.

Workaround: Use --allow-stale instead, or --sandbox mode.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T22:28:59.305898-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T00:54:20.335013-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T00:54:12.561872-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-5bj","title":"Registry has cross-process race condition","description":"The global daemon registry (~/.beads/registry.json) can be corrupted when multiple daemons from different workspaces write simultaneously.

**Root cause:**
- Registry uses an in-process mutex but no file-level locking
- Register() and Unregister() release the mutex between read and write
- Multiple daemon processes can interleave their read-modify-write cycles

**Evidence:**
Found registry.json with double closing bracket: `]]` instead of `]`

**Fix options:**
1. Use file locking (flock/fcntl) around the entire read-modify-write cycle
2. Use atomic write pattern (write to temp file, rename)
3. Both (belt and suspenders)

**Files:**
- internal/daemon/registry.go:46-64 (readEntries)
- internal/daemon/registry.go:67-87 (writeEntries)
- internal/daemon/registry.go:90-108 (Register - the race window)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-27T13:55:50.426188-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T14:07:06.22622-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T14:07:06.22622-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-5kj","title":"bd list fails in JSONL-only mode with misleading error","description":"When .beads exists with issues.jsonl but no SQLite (JSONL-only mode), bd list/ready/stats fail with no beads database found. However bd doctor works.  {"id":"bd-736d","title":"Refactor path canonicalization into helper function","description":"The path canonicalization logic (filepath.Abs + EvalSymlinks) is duplicated in 3 places:
- beads.go:131-137 (BEADS_DIR handling)
- cmd/bd/main.go:446-451 (--no-db cleanup)
- cmd/bd/nodb.go:26-31 (--no-db initialization)

Refactoring suggestion:
Extract to a helper function like:
  func canonicalizePath(path string) string

This would:
- Reduce code duplication
- Make the logic easier to maintain
- Ensure consistent behavior across all path handling

Related to bd-e16b implementation.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-11-02T18:33:47.727443-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T22:27:33.738672-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T22:27:33.738672-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-81a","title":"Add programmatic tip injection API","description":"Allow tips to be programmatically injected at runtime based on detected conditions. This enables dynamic tips (not just pre-defined ones) to be shown with custom priority and frequency.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-11T23:29:46.645583-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T17:52:35.096882-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T17:52:35.096882-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-81a","depends_on_id":"bd-d4i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-11T23:29:46.646327-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8a5","title":"Refactor: deduplicate FindJSONLInDir and FindJSONLPath","description":"## Background

After fixing bd-tqo, we now have two nearly identical functions for finding the JSONL file:
- `autoimport.FindJSONLInDir(dbDir string)` in internal/autoimport/autoimport.go
- `beads.FindJSONLPath(dbPath string)` in internal/beads/beads.go

Both implement the same logic:
1. Prefer issues.jsonl
2. Fall back to beads.jsonl for legacy support
3. Skip deletions.jsonl and merge artifacts
4. Default to issues.jsonl if nothing found

## Problem

Code duplication means bug fixes need to be applied in multiple places (as we just experienced with bd-tqo).

## Proposed Solution

Extract shared logic to a utility package that both can import. Options:
1. Create `internal/jsonlpath` package with the core logic
2. Have `autoimport` import `beads` and call `FindJSONLPath` (but APIs differ slightly)
3. Move to `internal/utils` if appropriate

Need to verify no import cycles would be created.

## Affected Files
- internal/autoimport/autoimport.go
- internal/beads/beads.go","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:45:18.974339-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T23:09:06.53707-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T23:07:08.912247-08:00"}
